extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked with 4.5km remaining after the GC group coalesced on the final climb, winning by 15 seconds while CARAPAZ took pink via earlier selection at 28.6km out.
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe blew apart the race with 80km to go using Aleotti/Kelderman, forcing splits before any classified climb.
Carapaz attacked at 28.6km out on descent/technical terrain creating 30-second gap, not on steepest gradient of Superga.
Nibali bridged with Hindley on final climb using tempo control rather than explosive attack, demonstrating veteran pacing strategy.
Yates given freedom with 4.5km left due to 20-minute GC deficit; GC riders refused to chase stage hunter.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: dropped in early Bora-Hansgrohe acceleration at 65km out, unable to follow pace on non-selective terrain
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: missed crucial split when Bora accelerated with 70km remaining, out of GC contention before climbing started
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: worked back after early split, finished within 39 seconds despite being gapped multiple times showing recovery resilience
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: bridged to Carapaz using controlled tempo on final climb, looked strongest since 2016 Giro Turin victory
rider_signals.LOPEZ Juanpe: negative: defended well initially but cracked when Carapaz attacked at 28.6km, losing 9 minutes and pink jersey
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: attacked on technical descent at 28.6km not summit, opened 30 seconds showing descent aggression mirrors 2019 win
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: followed Nibali's bridge effort and finished second, showing he can respond to varied pace changes not just steady climbing
